name: research
description: Multi-source parallel research with confidence-based synthesis.
qualityReason: Highly specific with clear synthesis logic
capabilities: Parallel web search using multiple query angles
Local documentation lookup in ai-docs directory
Three depth modes: quick (30s), standard (2min), extensive (8min)
Confidence scoring with HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, and CONFLICTING levels
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown workflow guides. All 53 static findings are false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code fences, not shell commands. The 'cryptographic' detections are SHA-256 content hashes in metadata. No executable code, no network requests, no filesystem operations.
valueStatement: Finding accurate information takes too long when checking multiple sources manually. This skill launches parallel research agents that search web, docs, and code simultaneously, then synthesize findings with confidence scores.
userTitle: Research any topic with multi-source verification
limitations: Requires WebSearch tool availability for web research
Cannot access paywalled or authenticated content
Local docs limited to ai-docs and specs directories
Extensive mode uses more resources and time

useCases.title: Find implementation patterns
useCases.description: Search codebase and web for how to implement specific features with verified code examples from multiple sources.

useCases.title: Research documentation topics
useCases.description: Gather information from web docs and local files to write accurate technical documentation quickly.

useCases.title: Make technology decisions
useCases.description: Use extensive mode to compare technologies and gather best practices for architecture decisions.

promptTemplates.title: Quick fact check
promptTemplates.scenario: Verify a simple fact in 30 seconds
promptTemplates.prompt: /ai-dev-kit:research quick What is the TOON format?

promptTemplates.title: Technical research
promptTemplates.prompt: /ai-dev-kit:research standard How to implement WebSocket authentication?

promptTemplates.title: Deep comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: /ai-dev-kit:research extensive Compare Codex CLI and Claude Code workflows

promptTemplates.title: Find code examples
promptTemplates.prompt: /ai-dev-kit:research standard state management implementation examples in codebase

bestPractices: Use quick mode for simple lookups and standard mode for most research tasks
Review the Conflicting Information section when confidence shows CONFLICTING
Verify critical findings by checking the listed source URLs and file paths
antiPatterns: Using extensive mode for simple fact checks wastes time and resources
Accepting LOW confidence answers without manual verification
Ignoring the Conflicting Information section in research reports

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All three have the WebSearch and file access tools this skill needs.

faq.question: How long does each research mode take?
faq.answer: Quick mode takes about 30 seconds. Standard mode takes about 2 minutes. Extensive mode takes about 8 minutes.

faq.question: What is the confidence scoring?
faq.answer: HIGH means 3+ sources agree. MEDIUM means 2 sources agree. LOW means only 1 source. CONFLICTING means sources disagree.

faq.question: What data leaves my machine during research?
faq.answer: Only your search queries are sent to web search endpoints. No personal data or local files are transmitted.

faq.question: What happens if web search fails?
faq.answer: The skill returns available results from local docs and code sources with a note about incomplete web coverage.

faq.question: Can I customize the research agents?
faq.answer: Yes. Add custom researcher types by creating agent definitions in the agents/research/ directory of your project.
